title: lespedeza japonica var. albiflora
intro: Lespedeza japonica var. albiflora, also known as White Desmodium or White Bush Clover, is a perennial legume native to East Asia, characterized by its white, pink, or purple flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial effects. Lespedeza japonica var. albiflora is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
